discovered_constraints: ed25519-dalek 2.1 API: VerifyingKey::verify_strict() not verify()
Signature::from_slice() returns Result, not Option
tokio::sync::RwLock has NO lock poisoning — .read().await / .write().await return guard directly, NOT Result

std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard is NOT Send — must be dropped before .await points in tonic gRPC handlers. Scope guard in a block { let guard = ...; ...; drop(guard); (result) } so it drops before the await
Workspace lints: all=deny, pedantic=warn, nursery=warn, unwrap_used=deny, expect_used=deny, panic=deny, unsafe_code=deny, todo=deny, unimplemented=deny
tonic in-process test pattern: TcpListener bind 127.0.0.1:0 → drop → serve_with_shutdown with oneshot channel
dyn IntegrationEvidenceEmitter does NOT implement Debug — cannot derive Debug on structs containing it. Use manual Debug impl with .field("emitter", &self.emitter.as_ref().map(|_| ".."))
INV-015: NO raw signatures, raw CVE feed payloads, or private keys in evidence payloads
IntegrationRecordType maps to aios_evidence::RecordType: StatusTransition, PolicyDecision, FailureObserved, ExternalBridgePackageAdmitted, ChainCheckpoint
Retention: VendorContractRevoked/ComplianceBaselineSnapshot/ControlMapDriftDetected = Forever; rest = Standard24M
Evidence emission is best-effort — errors from emitter are silently dropped (let _ = emitter.emit_...().await)
clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph (nursery) allowed at crate level in lib.rs — default 200-char limit too restrictive
CveId suffix must be all ASCII digits (>=4 chars) — is_valid_cve_id enforces CVE-YYYY-NNNN+ format
Arc::get_mut pattern for pre-clone mutation of authority registries
Orphan rule: test files cannot add inherent impl blocks for types defined in the crate
CveStatus enum: Open, UnderReview, Patched, Quarantined, NotApplicable — NO 'Active' variant
clippy::expect_used denied workspace-wide — MUST #[allow] on harness new() and register_vendor_authority()
clippy::missing_panics_doc (nursery) triggers on functions with expect() — must add # Panics section or #[allow]
clippy::items_after_statements: use statements inside function bodies must appear BEFORE any other statements; moving to top of fn body is the fix (NOT #[allow])
clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_calls: .map(|x| x.method()) can be .map(Type::method) when method takes &self
discovered_dependencies: IntegrationEvidenceEmitter trait is Option<Arc<dyn IntegrationEvidenceEmitter>> — when None, no emission occurs and no error is raised
WithIntegrationEmitter trait in evidence.rs for uniform builder pattern across 5 registries
ReceiptBuilder from aios_evidence crate requires subject, record_type, retention_class; seal(prev) produces chain-linked receipt
UnifiedRecordCatalogue is pre-populated via default_index_entries() which returns 64 CatalogueEntry values
last_errors: M19 parallel-factory race: a claude-ds T-196 worker (brief /tmp/t196-claudeds-brief.md) ran concurrently with the main session, both editing crates/aios-distribution. Factory committed its own T-196 (317f466) reverting my premature version bump to 0.0.1 and writing m19_closure.rs asserting 0.0.1. RESOLUTION: per user decision, let the factory finish T-196; then T-198 (the closer, my task) bumped 0.0.1->0.1.0 and reconciled m19_closure.rs to assert 0.1.0. History has duplicate T-196 (b72c224 mine + 317f466 factory) — harmless; 317f466 is the final state. LESSON: a milestone-closer task (version bump + version marker) must NOT run in parallel with a scope-limited evidence task on the same crate; the closer owns the version.

Version-marker authority: MILESTONES closure criterion #5 mandates 0.0.1->0.1.0 on the closing crate. A scoped worker that only adds evidence will leave version at 0.0.1 and may write a closure test asserting 0.0.1 — the closer must override both.
